key_insight: A self-supervised garment simulator can generate persistent wrinkles by treating plasticity as a moving energy-minimization problem and learning it with a physics-inspired curriculum.
engineering_relevance: This improves cloth realism for animation, virtual try-on, and digital humans without requiring supervised simulation data.
method: Replace purely elastic cloth loss with a physics-inspired elasto-plastic loss that encodes persistent wrinkles.
Train with curriculum learning that gradually shifts the target material from elasticity to elasto-plasticity.
Jointly optimize the changing loss function and model parameters so training converges despite nonstationary objectives.
evidence_signal: The authors report comprehensive evaluation showing natural persistent wrinkles and better performance than prior methods across multiple garments, body shapes, and body motions.
watch_item: Because the loss target changes during training, optimization can be unstable and may require careful curriculum scheduling and tuning.
paper_title: Self-supervised Garment Dynamics with Persistent Wrinkles
